---
layout: cv
title: Jakob Shurtleff's CV
---
# Jakob Shurtleff
Computational Physics Student, Programmer, Researcher.

<div id="webaddress">
<a href="mailto:jakobshurtleff@email.com">jakobshurtleff@email.com</a>
| <a href="https://github.com/shurtlej">My GitHub Profile</a>
| <a href="./resume.pdf">Download PDF</a>
</div>

## Currently

6th Semester Computational Physics student focusing on semiconductor theory and AI integration.

### Specialized in

Computational modeling, Python-based automation, multi-agent AI systems, and physics lab coordination.

### Research interests

Quantum mechanics, GAAFET/MOS capacitor simulation, astrophysics, and local data aggregation.


## Education

`Sept 2022 - Apr 2026`
**Brigham Young University–Idaho**, Rexburg, ID

- Bachelor of Science in Physics
- Computational Concentration


## Technical Skills

`Programming`
Python (Polars, Scipy, Lets-Plot), C++, Ollama, Web Scraping.

`Tools`
Linux, Git, Quarto, LaTeX, RTX-accelerated simulations.

`Languages`
English (Native), Mandarin Chinese, Thai.


## Projects

### Artificial Intelligence

`2026`
**Multi-Agent Chatbot**
Developed an interactive AI system using Python and Ollama for complex task delegation and reasoning.

`2026`
**AI Fish Simulation**
Designed and debugged a reward-based AI system for aquatic behavioral simulations in C++.

### Data Science

`2026`
**Local Event Scraper**
Built a Python-based web scraper to aggregate and format event data for Idaho Falls and Rexburg newsletters.


## Experience

`Feb 2026 - Present`
**Physics Lab Coordinator**, BYU-Idaho

- Managed laboratory equipment and experimental setups.
- Supported faculty in delivering undergraduate physics curriculum.

`2025 - 2026`
**Independent Developer**, Rexburg, ID

- Optimized hardware-specific environments for RTX 2070 systems.
- Developed and maintained custom Minecraft modpacks on NeoForge.
